title: KeeWeb vs Proton Pass for Minimalists
meta_description: KeeWeb vs Proton Pass for minimalists who want to open a local encrypted vault file in the browser without service accounts.
constraint_lens: Minimalists prefer tools that open a password vault file directly without requiring service accounts or hosted infrastructure.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for minimalists who want to open an encrypted password vault file directly in the browser.
one_second_verdict.reason: Proton Pass fails first because accessing the vault requires signing into a hosted account instead of opening a local encrypted database file.

verdict.summary: KeeWeb is the better choice for minimalists who want to access passwords without maintaining service accounts. It can open standard KDBX encrypted vault files directly inside the browser or desktop app without logging into any service. Proton Pass stores credentials inside a hosted vault tied to a Proton account. For users who want password access without service logins or hosted storage, requiring an account breaks the workflow.
verdict.decision_rule: If accessing the password vault requires signing into a hosted account service rather than opening a local encrypted database file, Proton Pass fails first.

sections.heading: Why KeeWeb fits minimalists
sections.content: The user wants to open an encrypted password vault file locally and avoid service accounts. KeeWeb loads standard KDBX database files directly in the browser or desktop interface. The vault remains a file that the user controls and stores in their own location such as local storage or personal cloud storage. This avoids creating accounts or relying on hosted password services.

bullets.point: KeeWeb opens standard KDBX encrypted database files directly in the browser interface.
bullets.why_it_matters: Users access passwords without signing into a service account.

bullets.point: KeeWeb reads vault files stored locally or in personal storage locations.
bullets.why_it_matters: The password vault remains a portable file controlled by the user.

bullets.point: KeeWeb operates without a hosted password service or account system.
bullets.why_it_matters: Minimalists avoid maintaining vendor accounts just to open a vault.

bullets.point: Proton Pass stores credentials in a hosted vault linked to a Proton account.
bullets.why_it_matters: Passwords sync automatically across devices after signing into the account.

bullets.point: Proton Pass browser extensions detect login forms and autofill credentials.
bullets.why_it_matters: Users can sign into websites quickly without opening a vault file manually.

bullets.point: Proton Pass restores the vault automatically when logging into a new device.
bullets.why_it_matters: Users regain access to credentials without importing database files.

items.fails_when: The user wants passwords synchronized automatically across devices without managing vault files.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Proton Pass where credentials sync through a hosted vault account.

items.fails_when: The user refuses service accounts because Proton Pass requires signing into a hosted vault.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use KeeWeb where the encrypted vault file opens locally without account login.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If the user prefers automatic syncing across devices and does not mind signing into an account service, Proton Pass may become the better option.

sections.heading: Quick decision rules
rules: Pick KeeWeb if you want to open an encrypted vault file without service accounts.
Pick KeeWeb if you keep your password database as a local KDBX file.
Pick Proton Pass if you want passwords synced automatically through a hosted vault.

faqs.a: KeeWeb opens encrypted vault files directly without requiring a service account.

faqs.q: Does Proton Pass require an account?
faqs.a: Yes. Proton Pass stores credentials in a hosted vault linked to a Proton account.

faqs.q: Can KeeWeb open standard KeePass vault files?
faqs.a: Yes. KeeWeb supports KDBX encrypted vault files used by KeePass compatible managers.

faqs.q: Who should choose Proton Pass instead?
faqs.a: Users who want automatic password syncing across devices may prefer Proton Pass.
